hawkish_phrases: monetary policy will need to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
key indicators of inflation persistence remained elevated
there was evidence of more persistent inflationary pressures than included within the forecast
measures of wage growth had moderated further but remained at rates above those consistent with the inflation target
services price inflation and wage growth have fallen by somewhat more than expected, key indicators of inflation persistence remain elevated
dovish_phrases: Headline CPI inflation has fallen back relatively sharply
The restrictive stance of monetary policy is weighing on activity in the real economy and is leading to a looser labour market
downside news has been broad-based, reflecting lower fuel, core goods and services price inflation
consumer price inflation was already, and had been for some time, on a firm downward trajectory
The outlook for demand remained weak, and less resilient than previously assumed
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to hold Bank Rate at 5.25%, with two members preferring a 25bp hike and one preferring a 25bp cut, maintaining a restrictive stance with emphasis on elevated inflation persistence despite falling headline inflation.
